description: A cut of beef cooked by grilling, pan-searing, broiling, or similar high-heat methods. Different cuts and degrees of doneness produce noticeably different texture and flavor.
preparation_steps: Bring the steak closer to room temperature and season it.
Sear over high heat or grill to the desired doneness.
Rest the steak briefly before slicing or serving.
Serve with simple sides or sauce if desired.

health_advice.benefits: Rich in protein and provides iron and vitamin B12.
health_advice.risks: Fat and calorie levels vary greatly by cut; charring and frequent large portions may be less desirable for routine intake.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose leaner cuts more often and pair with vegetables rather than heavy sides.

dietary_notes.note: Plain steak is naturally gluten-free, but sauces or marinades may introduce gluten.

allergens: Possible milk if butter is used
Possible gluten or soy in marinades or sauces
